---
title: "dpkg error code (1) - Can't install libgdal"
date: 2012-12-30
forum: New to Ubuntu
---

### Post by pavanmatham on 2012-12-30
Hi,

I was trying to install 'Open Geo Suite' on my Ubuntu 12.04. After downloading necessary things and started installing. After some time it started installing... shown up errors as[COLOR=Blue] attached [/COLOR]herewith.
[IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/data:image/jpeg;base64,[/IMG]Searched a lot for solution. Tried sudo apt-get install -f. It gave following error[IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/data:image/png;base64,[/IMG]

[COLOR=Blue]Reading package lists... Done
Building dependency tree       
Reading state information... Done
Correcting dependencies... Done
The following extra packages will be installed:
  libgdal
The following NEW packages will be installed:
  libgdal
0 upgraded, 1 newly installed, 0 to remove and 5 not upgraded.
3 not fully installed or removed.
Need to get 0 B/4,248 kB of archives.
After this operation, 12.8 MB of additional disk space will be used.
Do you want to continue [Y/n]? y
(Reading database ... 389472 files and directories currently installed.)
Unpacking libgdal (from .../libgdal_1.9.1-1_i386.deb) ...
dpkg: error processing /var/cache/apt/archives/libgdal_1.9.1-1_i386.deb (--unpack):
 trying to overwrite '/usr/bin/ogrinfo', which is also in package gdal-bin 1.9.2-2~precise4
No apport report written because MaxReports is reached already
                                                              dpkg-deb: error: subprocess paste was killed by signal (Broken pipe)
Errors were encountered while processing:
 /var/cache/apt/archives/libgdal_1.9.1-1_i386.deb
localepurge: Disk space freed in /usr/share/locale: 0 KiB
localepurge: Disk space freed in /usr/share/man: 0 KiB
localepurge: Disk space freed in /usr/share/gnome/help: 0 KiB
localepurge: Disk space freed in /usr/share/omf: 0 KiB

Total disk space freed by localepurge: 0 KiB[/COLOR]

E: Sub-process /usr/bin/dpkg returned an error code (1)
Please suggest a way of successful installation.

Thanks in advance.
Pavan

---

### Post by mc4man on 2012-12-30
Well it appears you're using this ppa (or have been..
[https://launchpad.net/~ubuntugis/+archive/ubuntugis-unstable/+packages?field.name_filter=&field.status_filter=published&field.series_filter=precise](https://launchpad.net/~ubuntugis/+archive/ubuntugis-unstable/+packages?field.name_filter=&field.status_filter=published&field.series_filter=precise)

You should be getting (or have already installed?? libgdal1_1.9.2-2_precise4_i386.deb which wouldn't conflict with gdal-bin 1.9.2-2~precise4 (notice that's libgdal[COLOR="Red"]1[/COLOR]

So make sure the ppa is still enabled, disable any ppa that's providing libgdal_1.9.1,  update your sources & get libgdal1_1.9.2-2 installed if not already, then try your Geo suite install

Or - if the geo suite is coming from a ppa & providing libgdal_1.9.1 then disable the ubuntugis ppa, remove all gdal packages (1.9.2), update sources & install the geo suite.

---
